What the full 7/16 session CONFIRMS is DIGESTION, not a break: the tape backed off the 7/15 after-hours high (SPY ~754.81, now -0.55% off it), QQQ 705.94 is STILL below its 50-day (-1.71%, mom5 -2.4) and never reclaimed it all session, and the extended leaders were the weak tape into the close (SPMO mom5 -5.1, VGT -2.95). The lone structural crack SLIPPED after-hours: MSFT 401.1 has faded back to -0.14% AT/just UNDER its 50-day (~401.7, mom5 +4.36 still positive) and stays bear-stacked, -8.52% below its 200-day — the tentative reclaim is fraying, not confirming. RRG confirms a DEFENSIVE rotation into the close: XLE is the cleanest rotation INTO (Improving ↑, accel +2.26, rsMom 102.48 board-best, one cross from Leading, +11.4% >200d); XLF is Leading (rsRatio 101.78, board-best); XLP Leading (accel +1.36), XLRE Leading (+1.16), XLU Improving — while XLI (Weakening ↓, accel -1.36) and XLV (Weakening ↓, accel -1.1) roll out. TACTICAL, last: gamma/OPEX walls and the live 7/29 FedWatch path are NOT in this run and must be re-pulled before sizing anything off positioning or the Fed. Plan for the next trading day: HOLD the bull-stack (do NOT add index beta overnight with QQQ below its 50-day into 7/17 OPEX); ROTATE toward XLE over rolling XLI; TRIM stretched, rolling semis into any relief; HOLD (don't chase) MSFT as its 50-day reclaim slips, into the 7/29 capex guide; carry a small defined-risk hedge overnight through 7/17 OPEX and toward the underpriced 7/29 hike tail.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Improving/Leading -> Weakening/Peaking) as a state-transition timing layer
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector crossing out of a strong quad (Leading->Peaking / Improving->Weakening) with negative acceleration on a fresh bar, then event-study forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the next scheduled catalyst confirms or denies the roll. THIS RUN the detector flags an XLI->XLE hand-off: XLI Weakening (quad W, accel -1.36, rsMom6m +1.64, rsMom 99.46) rolling out while XLE Improves (quad I, accel +2.26, rsMom 102.48, rsMom6m +14.21) rotates in, with leader XLK only Lagging (quad A, accel -0.54, rsMom1m -7.04) and no clean Leading GICS sector - a cyclical-to-cyclical (industrials->energy) hand-off, not yet a persistent leadership change (MA stack under XLE still tangled, +11.4% >200d), so it is actionable as a tilt but flagged for persistence per anti-whipsaw. The value is the state-transition TIMING, which no owning tool persists today. Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel/quad history across distinct bar dates + each sector's next dated catalyst + realized forward return.

experimental.title: Cross-tool haven-abstinence x sector-rotation risk-on gate (non-firing this run - logged as a negative observation)
experimental.note: Method: on each fresh bar, cross the real-assets monetary-haven leg against the sector-RRG rotation vector - declare a 'clean risk-on' state only when (a) BOTH monetary havens are in downtrends AND (b) a defensive sector is rotating out while a cyclical rotates in with no clean Leading defensive; then event-study forward bench drift conditioned on the joint state vs either half alone. THIS RUN the state does NOT fire: the haven leg is satisfied - GLD 9.4 and SLV 0 both Downtrend (mom63 -17.14 / -29.86), IBIT 24.3 Downtrend, no haven bid - but the sector roll is cyclical-to-cyclical (XLI industrials rotating OUT -> XLE energy rotating IN), so leg (b)'s defensive-out condition is unmet; XLV is not in the fresh into/out/leader triplet. Honestly a negative observation, useful only to distinguish a broad defensive-to-cyclical risk-on turn from an intra-cyclical reshuffle. Inputs: real-assets GLD/SLV/IBIT trend+mom63, sector per-name rrgState/rotation/accel with GICS defensive/cyclical tag, bench MA-stack; needs a persisted prior-bar snapshot to compute the state-transition delta across distinct bar dates.
